subject: March Must-Reads: Claude Skills, Contextual RAG, and More

Don't miss our most popular articles from the past month! ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­  

The Variable

March Must-Reads: Claude Skills, Contextual RAG, and More

Three months into 2026, it's becoming very clear that AI-assisted coding is the dominant theme of the year (so far, at least). 

 

From Claude Code skill-building to incorporating coding agents into existing workflows, data and ML practitioners are at the forefront of a rapid transformation. It comes with massive potential for efficiency and growth, but also for disruption and underwhelming results.

 

Our most-read articles of the past month reflect that excitement — and explore that tension. They also cover a broad range of other topics for those of you suffering from vibe coding fatigue. Let's dig in. 

Claude Skills and Subagents: Escaping the Prompt Engineering Hamster Wheel

Context bloat has become a real issue for product builders trying to automate and streamline repetiti elements within their work. Ruben Broekx shows how we can tackle this problem by leveraging the power of skills and subagents. He explains how they work, how they differ from MCP, and how all these disparate tools can come together into a cohesive workflow.

Read More

Together with Microsoft

Meet Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.6

Claude Opus 4.6 is redefining what’s possible. In this episode of Model Mondays, we demonstrate how adaptive thinking and context compaction power smarter AI applications helping teams accelerate development and scale with precision.

Watch Now

How to Build a Production-Ready Claude Code Skill

From a high-level overview we zoom in on the inner workings of Claude skills, with Hajime Takeda walking us through the process of building one from scratch. 

Read More

Understanding Context and Contextual Retrieval in RAG

Retrieval approaches have continued to evolve in recent months. Maria Mouschoutzi unpacks the role of context in boosting RAG performance.

Read More

Other March Highlights

Here are a few more of last month's most popular stories, offering practical insights on causal inference, multi-agent systems, and context engineering, among other topics:

  • How to Create Production-Ready Code with Claude Code, by Eivind Kjosbakken
    Learn how to write robust code with coding agents.
     

  • The Causal Inference Playbook: Advanced Methods Every Data Scientist Should Master, by Ananya Bhattacharyya

    Building the complete toolkit for real-world challenges

     

  • The Multi-Agent Trap, by Kaushik Rajan

    Adding more AI agents makes most systems worse. Three architecture patterns separate the $60M wins from the 40% that get canceled.

  • Self-Hosting Your First LLM, by Sam Black
    Privacy. Cost. Customization. Everything you need to know—step by step.

  • Context Engineering as Your Competitive Edge, by Dr. Janna Lipenkova
    If you have both unique domain expertise and know how to make it usable to your AI systems, you’ll be hard to beat. 

 

From our partner, Cautious Optimism

The market is shifting. Stay ahead of it.

Cautious Optimism is a newsletter that makes the connections people in tech actually need: how AI releases and tech developments intersect with market forces, policy shifts, and the power dynamics shaping the landscape.

Get the Newsletter

Authors in the Spotlight

We hope you take the time to read our recent author Q&A, and explore top-notch work from our newest contributors:

  • Emmimal P Alexander has been exploring a neuro-symbolic approach to fraud detection in a serious of deep dives.

  • Zairah Mustahsan devoted her debut article to AI search evaluation and how to improve it.

  • Our latest Author Spotlight is with Sara A. Metwalli, who chatted with us about quantum computing and why data professionals should care about it.

Contribute to TDS

Did your New Year's resolutions include publishing on TDS and joining our Author Payment Program? It's not too late to send along your draft!
Submit Your Article

Want more insights, industry trends, and exclusive content? Follow us on social media for real-time updates and expert discussions!
